candidates.why: Primary top-line revenue label; matches Total Revenue values and should be preferred as the main revenue line.
Duplicate of Revenue with identical values; kept as secondary priority in case label changes over time.
Finance income is non-operating and not appropriate for total revenue; excluded from final choice.
Interest income is non-operating and not appropriate for total revenue; excluded from final choice.
Explicitly non-operating subtotal, not revenue; excluded.
Bottom-line comprehensive income, not top-line revenue; excluded.
Comprehensive income measure, not revenue; excluded.

variables.notes: Revenue and Total Revenue have identical values; Revenue is treated as primary with Total Revenue as backup per priority rule.

candidates.why: Standard label for cost of goods sold / cost of sales; clearly the direct production cost line.
Compensation expense, more appropriately classified as SG&A rather than COGS; excluded for COGS.
Overhead/operating expenses, not direct cost of sales; excluded for COGS.
Operating rental cost, usually overhead; not specifically COGS.
Additional operating overhead; not direct COGS.
Financial expense, not COGS.
Supplemental disclosure of rental expense, not core COGS.
Inventory component on the balance sheet, not an income statement expense line; excluded.
Liability account, not an expense classification for COGS.

variables.notes: Cost of goods sold is clearly the direct cost of sales line and best match for COGS.

candidates.why: Personnel-related operating overhead; typical SG&A component, but an explicit SG&A aggregate line exists and is populated, so this is not selected per hierarchy rule.
Stock-based compensation, usually part of SG&A; however, an explicit SG&A line exists and is populated, so we use only that aggregate per instructions.
Additional personnel-related overhead; would be SG&A but is superseded by the explicit SG&A aggregate line.
Operating overhead bucket; candidate SG&A component but superseded by explicit SG&A line per rule (a).
Residual operating overhead; also superseded by explicit SG&A line.
Explicit SG&A line, populated in preview; per hierarchy rule (a), this single row should be used for XSGA_COMPONENTS.
Equity component on balance sheet, not a P&L expense line; excluded from SG&A components.

variables.notes: There is a clear, populated SG&A line ('Selling, general, and administrative'); per instructions, only this explicit SG&A row is selected for XSGA_COMPONENTS, even though detailed overhead components are also listed.
No row in the provided Income Statement or Balance Sheet clearly corresponds to research and development expense; it appears R&D is either not separately disclosed or not present, so XRD is set to 0.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Explicit interest expense related to lease liabilities; valid interest expense component, but more specific than the generic interest expense line.
Primary generic interest expense line; best overall measure of interest expense and should be first in priority list.
Represents minority shareholders’ share of income, not interest expense; excluded.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease-related interest; can serve as a fallback if main lines are missing.
Equity account for non-controlling interests, not interest expense; excluded.
Minority equity account, not an expense; excluded.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Interest Expense is the main interest cost; lease-related interest lines are provided as fallbacks in case of label changes or partial disclosures over time.

candidates.row_label: Non-current investments other than investments accounted for using equity method
candidates.why: An asset account (non-current investments), not equity; inappropriate for book equity.
Potential equity component but appears unused (nan) and is not the main total equity measure; not chosen for BE.
Primary total shareholders’ equity line; no separate parent/owner equity subtotal is provided, so this is the best proxy for book equity excluding minority interests (which are reported separately).
Represents non-controlling interests, not the parent’s equity; used for MIB instead of BE.
Duplicate labeling of Total equity with same values; only one is needed to avoid duplication.

variables.notes: No explicit 'equity attributable to owners/parent' line exists; Total equity is used for BE, while minority interests are captured separately via MIB to avoid double counting.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit/loss to non-controlling interests; can serve as a fallback indicator for MIB if balance sheet lines are missing.
Balance sheet line for minority/non-controlling interests in equity; primary choice for MIB as it represents the minority equity component directly.
Alternative or legacy label for non-controlling interests in equity; good secondary fallback for MIB.
Income statement total of profit/loss attributable to minority interests; tertiary fallback for MIB if balance sheet info were unavailable.

variables.notes: Primary measure of minority interests comes from the balance sheet equity line; additional labels are provided as fallbacks to handle possible label changes or reporting differences over time.
